resources.string: Maintains the workspace connection when the screen is off. Shows a persistent notification.
Shown when the keep-alive service is running to maintain the workspace connection.
The WebView renderer process terminated unexpectedly. Your session state may have been lost.
The VS Code Tunnel URL to connect to. Must use HTTPS.
When enabled, a foreground service with a persistent notification maintains the connection while the screen is off.
Open Android app settings (battery, permissions, notifications)
Request to disable battery optimization for this app
Enter the VS Code Tunnel workspace URL:
Invalid URL. Must start with https://
The workspace URL will be reset to the default tunnel endpoint.

TunnelKeep is a native Android client for accessing VS Code Tunnel workspaces. It provides foreground keep-alive, connectivity recovery, and session preservation features designed for mobile workflows.
TunnelKeep is not affiliated with or endorsed by Microsoft, GitHub, OpenAI, or Anthropic. It is an independent utility for personal use. Use at your own risk.

To keep TunnelKeep running reliably on HyperOS:

1. App Info → Battery saver → No restrictions
Long-press TunnelKeep → App info → Battery saver → select "No restrictions".

2. Autostart
App Info → Autostart → enable. This reduces the chance that HyperOS blocks background operation; TunnelKeep does not automatically start itself after reboot.

3. Lock app in recents
Open recent apps → long-press TunnelKeep → tap the lock icon.

4. Security → App lock (optional)
Settings → Apps → App lock → disable for TunnelKeep if you want faster access.

5. Developer options (if available)
Under "Don't keep activities" → ensure this is OFF.

These steps reduce the chance of HyperOS killing the keep-alive service. Real-world behavior varies by device and ROM version.

• Screen-off behavior: A partial wake lock keeps the CPU running, but some devices may still suspend WebView rendering. VS Code\'s own reconnect logic handles most interruptions.

• No offline editing: The app requires an active internet connection to the VS Code Tunnel server.

• Single workspace: Currently supports one workspace URL at a time.

• Battery impact: The keep-alive service consumes additional battery. Disable it when not needed.

• File synchronization: File changes made in the web editor are saved to the remote machine via the tunnel; local device storage is not used.
